title: OREGON STATE SUMMER FOOD SERVICE PROGRAM - 211info
text: OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION OREGON STATE SUMMER FOOD SERVICE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides free, nutritious meals and snacks to help children in low-income areas get the nutrition they need throughout the summer months. Offered by approved sponsors, including school districts, local government agencies, camps, and private nonprofit organizations. Summer meal programs may serve a combination of breakfast, lunch, snack, or supper. Some programs also offer learning activities for kids before and after meals. 255 Capitol Street NE Salem, OR 97310 Eligibility: Ages 1-18 Hours: Varies by meal site Email (503) 947-5600 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 947-5600 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit the website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Summer Food Service Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER ASSISTANCE AND PROTECTION - OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - OREGON SCHOOL DIRECTORY EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - MCKINNEY-VENTO HOMELESS EDUCATION ASSISTANCE EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - MIGRANT EDUCATION PROGRAM EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - TEEN PARENT SUCCESS PROGRAM FOOD - CHILD AND ADULT CARE FOOD PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION
